should_trigger: Design an auto-orchestrator where an agent updates job state, emits a queue message, and calls a webhook, but crashes and retries must not create lost or duplicate work.
Our workflow claims exactly-once execution across a database and message broker. Specify the transaction boundary, idempotency keys, outbox/inbox records, and recovery loop.
Two workers can race to claim and finalize the same case. Repair the orchestration with isolation, version checks, leases, or fencing tokens.
Build a saga for a multi-step agent workflow with payment, document generation, notification, and rollback-like compensation for each irreversible step.
A publisher sometimes sends an event but crashes before marking it sent. Use transaction-processing theory to make publication and consumer processing safe.
should_not_trigger: Create a static dependency graph for a four-step content workflow with no shared state, retries, messages, or side effects.
Summarize the history of database transaction processing in one paragraph without applying it to an orchestrator design.
Improve the visual layout of a dashboard for monitoring workflow runs.
edge_cases: The workflow has one database write and one email send. Decide whether a full saga is overkill, and still specify the minimum idempotency and recovery rules.
The team wants to serialize every agent action to avoid race conditions. Decide which invariants need strong transaction boundaries and which steps can remain eventually consistent.
A queue vendor advertises exactly-once delivery. Evaluate whether the orchestrator still needs deduplication, idempotency keys, and durable state transitions.

compliance_expectations.should_trigger: Applies the transaction-processing gate, identifies invariant, durable state, side effects, commit boundaries, idempotency, outbox/inbox or saga needs, recovery loop, compensation, isolation, and crash/race tests.
compliance_expectations.should_not_trigger: Does not activate for static workflows without shared durable state, retries, messages, races, or external side effects.
compliance_expectations.edge_cases: Scopes down to minimum safe idempotency/recovery when a full saga is excessive and challenges exactly-once or serialize-everything claims with explicit invariants.
